question_title: How many #1 seeds will reach the Elite Eight in the NCAA's 2026 March Madness?
summary: Based on 40 years of NCAA tournament data (1985-2025), the historical distribution of #1 seeds reaching the Elite Eight is: 0→2.5%, 1→7.5%, 2→32.5%, 3→32.5%, 4→25.0%. The per-seed probability of reaching the E8 is 67.5% (108/160). The tournament hasn't started yet, so this is purely a base-rate question. I use a blend of the empirical distribution (which captures overdispersion/correlation between seed outcomes) and the binomial model for smoothing. The mode is 3, with 2 nearly as likely. Recent years show slightly more upset tendency but the sample is small.

factors.description: Historical base rate: Over 40 tournaments (1985-2025), the empirical distribution is 0→2.5%, 1→7.5%, 2→32.5%, 3→32.5%, 4→25%. Mean is 2.70.
Per-seed probability of reaching E8 is 67.5% (108/160). Binomial(4, 0.675) mode is 3 at 40%, supporting 3 as most likely outcome.
Recent decade (2015-2025) shows more upset volatility: mean dropped to 2.30, with the first-ever 0-seed year (2023). This weakly favors lower outcomes.
No team-specific information available yet (tournament hasn't started). Pure base rate question with no directional adjustment.
Overdispersion in empirical data vs binomial: tournament-level factors create correlation, producing fatter tails (more 0s and 4s than binomial predicts).

reasoning:
**Forecast: How many #1 seeds will reach the Elite Eight in 2026 March Madness?**

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|------------|
| 0 | 2% |
| 1 | 8% |
| 2 | 31% |
| 3 | 35% |
| 4 | 24% |

**Key reasoning:**
- Based on 40 years of tournament data (1985-2025), the per-seed probability of reaching the Elite Eight is **67.5%** (108/160 individual #1 seeds).
- The historical distribution peaks at **2 and 3** (each occurring 32.5% of the time), with 4 at 25%.
- Only once (2023) did zero #1 seeds reach the E8. Three years saw exactly 1.
- The tournament hasn't started yet — Selection Sunday is mid-March — so no team-specific adjustments are possible. This is a pure base-rate forecast.
- I blend the empirical distribution (which captures real-world overdispersion from correlated seed outcomes) with a binomial model for smoothing.

resolution_reason: The 2026 NCAA Division I men's basketball tournament Elite Eight (Regional Finals / Round of 8) has been determined. Three #1 seeds reached the Elite Eight: Duke (#1 East), Michigan (#1 Midwest), and Arizona (#1 West). The fourth #1 seed, Florida (South Region), was eliminated in the second round by #9 Iowa. The Elite Eight matchups are: Duke vs UConn (East), Michigan vs Tennessee (Midwest), Arizona vs Purdue (West), and Illinois vs Iowa (South). The Sweet 16 games completed on March 26-27, 2026, establishing the Elite Eight field.
resolution_criteria: This question will resolve as the amount of Number 1 bracket seeds that qualify for the Regional Finals (Round of 8) in the [2026 NCAA Division I men's basketball tournament](https://www.ncaa.com/march-madness-live/bracket).
fine_print:
If the tournament's format is changed or interrupted, this question will resolve based upon the final eight teams.

If the tournament is cancelled or delayed such that results remain unknown before May 1, 2026, this question will be annulled.

***
This question's information (resolution criteria, fine print, background info, etc) is synced with an [original identical question](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42290) which opened on 2026-02-25 17:00:00. This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print contained above. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.
